children.name: Oatmeal — Lemon-Herb Set
children.short_description: Creamy oatmeal with banana and almonds for a satisfying, wholesome breakfast.
children.meal_suggestion: Enjoy warm as a filling breakfast bowl. To echo the Lemon-Herb theme, add a splash of lemon zest and herbs, or top with fresh berries for extra flavor and antioxidants.
children.nutrition_note: High in fiber from oats, protein from milk and almonds, and natural sugars from banana. Provides calcium and potassium with healthy fats; a balanced, satiating breakfast.

children.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Berry Boost Bake
children.short_description: A quick, balanced plate of mixed fruit, nuts, and yogurt with a berry-boosted flavor profile.
children.meal_suggestion: Portion idea: 1 cup mixed fruit, 1/2 cup yogurt, and a small handful (about 1 oz / 28 g) of nuts. Add cinnamon or a light drizzle of honey if desired. For higher protein, use Greek yogurt.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber- and vitamin-rich fruit paired with yogurt provides protein and calcium; nuts add healthy fats and satiety. Natural sugars come from the fruit, so watch portions if you're monitoring calories.

children.name: Banana Bread Toast — Classic Bowl
children.short_description: Banana bread toast topped with Greek yogurt and berries—a quick, balanced breakfast with protein, carbs, and fiber.
children.meal_suggestion: Toast a slice of banana bread, spread with Greek yogurt, and top with a handful of fresh berries.
children.nutrition_note: Balanced mix of carbs, protein, and micronutrients: banana bread provides energy; Greek yogurt supplies protein and calcium; berries deliver fiber and antioxidants. For a lighter option, use whole-grain banana bread and low-fat yogurt.

children.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Garlic-Ginger Variation
children.short_description: A vegetarian quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa. Fiber and calcium-rich with veggie nutrients, ready in a quick, balanced meal.
children.meal_suggestion: Pair with a light side salad or fruit to round out the meal; for higher protein, add a lean protein (beans, tofu, or chicken) or include more beans inside the quesadilla; using reduced-fat cheese can lower overall saturated fat.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; provides calcium and protein from cheddar; includes vitamins A, C, and K from peppers and spinach; salsa adds vitamin C. Watch sodium from cheese and salsa; consider reduced-fat cheese or low-sodium salsa to lower saturated fat and sodium.

children.name: Caprese Sandwich — Mild-Spice Bowl
children.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich with whole-grain bread, mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic for a mild, fresh flavor.
children.meal_suggestion: For a bowl version, serve components over greens or quinoa and drizzle with balsamic. To boost protein, add grilled chicken or chickpeas. Pair with fruit or veggie side to round out the meal and keep portions mindful of sodium.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread plus protein and calcium from mozzarella; tomatoes and basil add vitamins. Balsamic adds flavor with modest sugar; watch sodium from cheese. Mild, vegetarian option.

children.name: Egg Fried Rice — Mild-Spice Bake
children.short_description: A baked, mildly spiced version of egg fried rice with rice, egg, peas, carrots, soy sauce and sesame oil.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve warm with a side of leafy greens or cucumber salad to balance sodium; for fiber boost, substitute brown rice and opt for low-sodium soy sauce.
children.nutrition_note: Protein from egg and carbs from rice; includes fiber and micronutrients from peas and carrots. Sesame oil adds healthy fats. Soy sauce adds sodium—use low-sodium soy sauce or reduce amount. For more fiber, swap white rice with brown rice.

children.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Lemon-Herb Wrap
children.short_description: Fiber-rich, plant-forward meal: vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad.
children.meal_suggestion: To increase protein and fullness, add a protein source (beans, lentils, yogurt, or grilled poultry). Use a low-sodium soup option and drizzle a small amount of olive oil on the salad for healthy fats. Consider adding a piece of fruit for dessert.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich meal from vegetables and whole grains; provides complex carbs, vitamins, and minerals with low saturated fat. Sodium varies with soup—opt for a low-sodium option to keep sodium reasonable.

children.name: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
children.short_description: Lean beef tacos on corn tortillas with fresh veggies and cheddar—balanced, customizable DIY taco night.
children.meal_suggestion: Assemble 2 warm corn tortillas and fill with 3–4 oz lean ground beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, a tablespoon of salsa, and a light sprinkle of cheddar. Optional: top with avocado for healthy fats.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich lean beef with fiber from lettuce, tomato, salsa and corn tortillas; calcium from cheddar. Monitor sodium from salsa and cheese. For a lighter option, use 2 tortillas, 3–4 oz beef, and low-sodium salsa or reduced-fat cheddar.

children.name: DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet
children.short_description: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
children.meal_suggestion: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
children.nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.

children.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Smoky Combo
children.short_description: A balanced snack of mixed fruit, yogurt, and nuts delivering fiber, protein, and healthy fats.
children.meal_suggestion: Assemble a serving with 1 cup mixed fruit, ¼–½ cup plain Greek yogurt, and a small handful (about ¼ cup) nuts; enjoy as a portable, balanced snack and adjust portions to fit your daily needs.
children.nutrition_note: Provides fiber and antioxidants from fruit, protein and calcium from yogurt, and healthy fats from nuts. To keep sugar and calories in check, choose plain yogurt and unsalted nuts.

children.name: Overnight Oats — Citrus Fresh Twist
children.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with milk, plain yogurt, apple and cinnamon; a protein- and fiber-rich breakfast that can be brightened with a citrus note.
children.meal_suggestion: For a true Citrus Fresh Twist, add citrus zest (orange or lemon) or a small splash of juice to brighten the oats. Top with chopped nuts or seeds for healthy fats and extra crunch. Consider vanilla yogurt or a touch of vanilla extract for depth.
children.nutrition_note: Balanced breakfast with protein and calcium from yogurt and milk, plus fiber from oats and apple. No added sugar in the base ingredients; cinnamon adds flavor and may support blood sugar regulation. Calories vary with dairy choices but it's a filling, nutrient-dense start to the day.

children.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Citrus Fresh Variation
children.short_description: Whole-grain pancakes with egg, milk, berries, and maple syrup—fiber-rich, protein-containing breakfast with natural sweetness.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve with a small yogurt or extra berries for added protein or fiber. To echo the citrus theme, garnish with orange zest or add a splash of citrus juice to the batter.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich whole-wheat batter with egg and milk provides sustained energy and high-quality protein; berries add vitamins, fiber, and antioxidants; maple syrup adds sweetness with natural sugars. The Citrus Fresh Variation name suggests optional citrus zest or juice for extra vitamin C and bright flavor, not included in the base ingredients.

children.name: Egg Fried Rice — Maple-Kissed Skillet
children.short_description: Savory egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of steamed greens or add tofu/chicken for extra protein. Try brown rice for more fiber. Use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate sesame oil to reduce sodium and fat.
children.nutrition_note: A balanced quick fried rice dish: carbohydrates from the rice, protein from the egg, and fiber from peas and carrots. Contains healthy fats from sesame oil. Sodium can be high due to soy sauce; consider low-sodium soy sauce or reducing the amount. For more fiber, swap to brown rice; for more protein, add another egg or lean protein on the side.

children.name: Hummus Snack Plate — Citrus Fresh Style
children.short_description: Colorful Mediterranean-inspired snack plate featuring hummus, whole-wheat pita, veggies, and a cheese component for protein, fiber, and healthy fats.
children.meal_suggestion: As a Citrus Fresh-style snack: assemble with 2–3 tablespoons hummus, 1 small whole-wheat pita cut into wedges, cucumber slices, cherry tomatoes, and a small cheese wedge. Add a squeeze of lemon or citrus zest to brighten the flavors. If dairy-free, omit cheese or substitute with a plant-based option.
children.nutrition_note: This plate provides protein from hummus and cheese, plus fiber from whole-wheat pita and vegetables, and healthy fats from tahini in hummus. It offers a balanced mix of complex carbs, protein, and fats. Watch sodium from store-bought hummus and cheese; consider reduced-sodium options or smaller portions if needed. For a lighter version, you can reduce cheese or use part-skim options.

children.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Classic Bake
children.short_description: A baked veggie quesadilla with whole-wheat tortilla, cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side salad or fruit. For a lighter version, use part-skim cheese or reduce cheese amount; add avocado for healthy fats and extra fiber.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; protein and calcium from cheddar. Spinach and bell pepper supply vitamins A and C; salsa adds flavor with relatively few calories. If watching sodium, choose low-sodium cheese or salsa.

children.name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Berry Boost Combo
children.short_description: Turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in marinara, topped with parmesan; Berry Boost indicates a berry-based fruit side for added antioxidants.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of mixed berries or a small berry compote to fit the Berry Boost concept; add steamed greens for extra fiber and micronutrients.
children.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides high-quality protein; whole-wheat pasta adds fiber and complex carbs; marinara delivers tomatoes and flavor; parmesan contributes calcium. The Berry Boost name suggests pairing with a berry fruit side for extra antioxidants and vitamins.

children.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Twist
children.short_description: Roasted chicken dinner with quinoa and vegetables (broccoli and carrots), adaptable with a berry-enhanced twist for extra antioxidants.
children.meal_suggestion: Suggested portion: 4–6 oz cooked chicken thighs; 1/2–3/4 cup cooked quinoa; 1 cup broccoli; 1 cup carrots. Optional: include a side of berries or a berry glaze to fit the Berry Boost Twist.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich with chicken thighs and quinoa; fiber and vitamins from broccoli and carrots. Berry Boost Twist can be realized by adding fresh berries or a berry sauce on the side for extra antioxidants.

children.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Garden Veg Style
children.short_description: Sheet-pan pork dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, green beans, and olive oil.
children.meal_suggestion: Roast pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans on a sheet pan, drizzled with olive oil and seasoned with garlic and herbs for a simple, nutritious dinner.
children.nutrition_note: Lean pork provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbs and potassium; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a balanced portion, aim for about 4–6 oz pork with 1–2 cups vegetables and a moderate potato serving per person.

children.name: Banana Bread Toast — Sesame Combo
children.short_description: Banana bread toast topped with yogurt and berries for a protein-fiber balanced snack.
children.meal_suggestion: Toast a slice of banana bread; spread with Greek yogurt; top with fresh berries. If desired, sprinkle a small amount of sesame seeds for crunch and extra minerals.
children.nutrition_note: Protein: moderate from Greek yogurt; carbs from banana bread and berries; fiber from berries (and banana bread depending on recipe). Fat varies with yogurt type; watch added sugars in banana bread. This combo provides calcium and vitamins from dairy and fruit; suitable as a quick breakfast or snack.

children.name: Apple-Cinnamon Porridge — Maple-Kissed Variation
children.short_description: Creamy apple-cinnamon porridge made with oats and milk, with an optional maple-sweetened variation.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a comforting breakfast. For extra protein, top with yogurt or nuts; if you want the maple-kissed variation, swirl in a small amount of maple syrup.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich oats and apple support fullness; milk adds protein and calcium; cinnamon adds flavor with minimal added sugar. Maple-kissed variation suggests a light maple sweetness—add maple syrup sparingly if desired.

children.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Mild-Spice Plate
children.short_description: Whole-grain pancakes with berries and maple syrup—fiber-rich, protein-present, and naturally sweet with a touch of mild spice.
children.meal_suggestion: Pair with Greek yogurt or additional berries for extra protein and fiber. Serve warm with a light drizzle of maple syrup; optionally add a pinch of cinnamon for a mild spice note.
children.nutrition_note: High-fiber, protein-containing plate from whole-wheat flour, egg, and milk; antioxidants from berries; natural sugars from maple syrup. To limit added sugars, drizzle sparingly and consider lighter syrup or fruit topping.

children.name: Lentil Soup — Garden Veg Bowl
children.short_description: A hearty lentil soup with carrots, celery, and onions in broth, paired with whole-grain bread for a balanced plant-based meal.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve hot lentil-vegetable soup with a slice of toasted whole-grain bread; consider a squeeze of lemon or fresh herbs for brightness.
children.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils; vegetables add vitamins and minerals; whole-grain bread boosts filling carbohydrates and fiber. If sodium is a concern, use low-sodium broth and monitor portion size.

children.name: Grilled Cheese & Soup — Classic Skillet
children.short_description: Classic skillet grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, tomato soup, and grapes.
children.meal_suggestion: Consider adding a side salad or steamed vegetables to boost veggie intake; use a smaller slice of cheddar or a lower-fat cheese to reduce saturated fat; choose a low-sodium tomato soup to help manage sodium.
children.nutrition_note: Provides protein from cheddar and fiber from whole-grain bread, plus antioxidants from grapes. Tomato soup adds volume but can be high in sodium; opt for low-sodium soup and moderate cheese portions for a balanced meal.

children.name: Grilled Cheese & Soup — Garlic-Ginger Bake
children.short_description: Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, tomato soup, and grapes.
children.meal_suggestion: Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, served with tomato soup and a side of grapes. To make it lighter, choose reduced-sodium soup and a smaller cheese portion; add a simple side salad for more vegetables.
children.nutrition_note: Balanced mix: complex carbs from whole-grain bread and grapes, protein and calcium from cheddar, and vegetables from tomato soup. Tomato soup can be high in sodium; opt for low-sodium or homemade if possible. Grapes add fruit and fiber; consider adding extra veggies to boost fiber and micronutrients.

children.name: Bean & Cheese Burritos — Garlic-Ginger Bake
children.short_description: Warm garlic-ginger baked burritos with black beans, cheddar, salsa, and greens in whole-wheat tortillas.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve with extra greens or fresh salsa; add a piece of fruit on the side. For a lighter version, use reduced-fat cheese or low-sodium salsa; to boost protein, add a dollop of Greek yogurt on the side.
children.nutrition_note: High in fiber and plant-based protein from black beans and whole-wheat tortillas; calcium from cheddar; greens add vitamins and minerals. The dish can be higher in sodium from cheese and salsa, so consider low-sodium cheese/salsa or add more vegetables to increase volume with less salt.

children.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Sesame Twist
children.short_description: Wholesome whole-wheat crust pizza with tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell peppers; sesame twist adds extra flavor and fats if used.
children.meal_suggestion: Top with sesame seeds (optional); choose part-skim mozzarella; load with peppers and mushrooms; serve with a simple green salad.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat crust; calcium and protein from mozzarella; lycopene from tomato sauce; vitamins A and C from bell peppers; mushrooms add minerals. Optional sesame seeds provide healthy fats. For lower sodium, use reduced-sodium sauce and lighter cheese; pair with a side salad for more veggies.

children.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Garlic-Ginger Bake
children.short_description: Baked stuffed bell peppers filled with ground turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve 1–2 stuffed peppers per person with a green salad or steamed vegetables; for lower calories, use brown rice and reduced-fat cheddar; can be made ahead and reheated.
children.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; rice gives carbs; peppers add fiber and vitamins; cheddar supplies calcium and fat. Tomato sauce adds lycopene; sodium varies by sauce—use low-sodium options to reduce sodium.

children.name: Breakfast Wrap — Citrus Fresh Bake
children.short_description: A quick, balanced breakfast wrap with a whole-wheat tortilla, egg, turkey, spinach, and salsa.
children.meal_suggestion: Pair with a piece of fruit (e.g., orange) for extra vitamin C, and consider a squeeze of lime or a bit of avocado for freshness. To reduce sodium, use low-sodium turkey and homemade salsa.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich from egg and turkey; fiber and micronutrients from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach; salsa adds flavor and vitamin C. Mind sodium: turkey and salsa can be high-sodium—choose low-sodium turkey or add extra veggies to boost volume.

children.name: Breakfast Wrap — Lemon-Herb Skillet
children.short_description: A warm breakfast wrap featuring a whole-wheat tortilla, scrambled egg, turkey slices, spinach, and salsa, cooked in a lemon-herb skillet.
children.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced breakfast wrap with protein, fiber, and vegetables; pair with fruit or yogurt for a fuller morning meal.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich meal thanks to egg and turkey; fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach; salsa adds flavor with minimal added sugar. Sodium can be modest-to-high depending on turkey and salsa—opt for low-sodium versions if needed. For more fiber, add extra greens or use a higher-fiber tortilla; for lower fat, use egg whites and a smaller tortilla.

children.name: Breakfast Wrap — Smoky Combo
children.short_description: Protein-packed breakfast wrap with egg, turkey, spinach, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla.
children.meal_suggestion: Suggestions: swap turkey for chicken or plant-based protein; add avocado for healthy fats; include additional colorful veggies (bell peppers, onions); use low-sodium salsa or serve as a bowl to vary texture.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast with egg and turkey, plus fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach. Includes vegetables and salsa; watch sodium levels from turkey and salsa—choose low-sodium options or reduce added salt. Optional avocado adds healthy fats.

children.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Combo
children.short_description: A comforting chicken noodle soup with carrots and celery in a light broth, finished with a citrus-bright touch.
children.meal_suggestion: Tips: switch to low-sodium broth and whole-grain noodles for more fiber; add leafy greens (spinach or kale) for extra nutrients; finish with lemon juice or zest to enhance the citrus note and aroma.
children.nutrition_note: Lean chicken provides protein; noodles deliver carbohydrates; carrots and celery add fiber and micronutrients; broth contributes hydration and sodium (use low-sodium broth to reduce sodium). To honor the Citrus Fresh theme, finish with a squeeze of lemon juice or lemon zest for vitamin C and brightness without added fat.

children.name: Turkey Sandwich — Mild-Spice Combo
children.short_description: A mild-spice turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard.
children.meal_suggestion: Keep as a simple, balanced lunch: turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce and tomato. Pair with fruit or carrot sticks for more fiber. To adjust spice, use a milder mustard or add a small amount of hot sauce if acceptable.
children.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; whole-grain bread adds fiber; lettuce and tomato supply vitamins. Moderate sodium from deli turkey and mustard; consider low-sodium options if needed. Contains gluten (bread).

children.name: Egg Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Skillet
children.short_description: Egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil; a quick protein- and veggie-packed skillet meal (lemon-herb flavor notes optional).
children.meal_suggestion: One-pan dish ready in minutes. Serve as is or with a side salad or steamed greens. To adjust nutrition, swap in brown rice for more fiber and/or reduce oil; use low-sodium soy sauce to cut sodium. Optional lemon zest and herbs can brighten the flavor.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich egg with rice provides a balanced mix of carbs and protein; vegetables add fiber and micronutrients (vitamin A from carrots, vitamin C from peas). Sesame oil contributes flavor and healthy fats; soy sauce increases sodium, so consider low-sodium soy sauce or reduce amount. For more fiber, use brown rice or add extra veggies; for brighter flavor, add lemon zest/juice and fresh herbs (optional).

children.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bowl
children.short_description: Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, broccoli, and carrots for a protein-forward, fiber-rich veggie bowl.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve warm: roasted chicken thighs with fluffy quinoa and steamed broccoli and carrots; the name suggests a maple-kissed flavor—top with a light maple glaze if you like.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken thighs provide satiety; quinoa offers complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots add vitamins A and C plus fiber, making this a balanced bowl with moderate fat from the thighs.

children.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Bowl
children.short_description: A fiber-forward garden veggie meal featuring vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a fresh side salad.
children.meal_suggestion: Great as a light, balanced lunch or dinner. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or hummus to the soup or salad; drizzle a little olive oil on the salad for healthy fats; choose low-sodium soup to manage salt intake.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich, plant-based meal with vegetables, whole grains, and a side of greens. Provides complex carbs from whole-grain bread and essential vitamins; protein is modest—consider adding a protein source if you need more satiety. Watch sodium levels in soup; opt for low-sodium options when possible.

children.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Sesame Skillet
children.short_description: One-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans roasted in olive oil.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve as a complete dinner: pair with a side salad or steamed greens and a grain (e.g., quinoa) for extra fiber. Season with herbs and a light seasoning of salt if desired.
children.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. This one-pan meal can fit into a balanced diet when portions are appropriate.

children.name: Miso Breakfast Set — Sesame Variation
children.short_description: Miso breakfast set with tofu, egg, green onion, rice, and optional sesame variation for a protein-packed savory morning meal.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a balanced bowl: mix in the tofu with a small amount of miso for flavor, top with green onion and a gently cooked egg, and accompany with steamed rice. If sesame variation is used, finish with toasted sesame seeds for extra texture; opt for low-sodium miso to keep sodium in check.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast from tofu and egg supports satiety and muscle maintenance. Includes rice for steady energy; miso provides fermentation-associated flavor and potential gut benefits. Sodium intake can be high from miso paste—consider low-sodium miso if needed. Sesame variation may add healthy fats and a nutty flavor if sesame is used.

children.name: Breakfast Sandwich — Sesame Bowl
children.short_description: A savory breakfast sandwich on a whole-wheat English muffin with egg, cheddar, and tomato.
children.meal_suggestion: Quick, balanced breakfast. To adjust: add another egg or more cheese for extra protein; use egg whites and light cheese to lower calories; choose a higher-fiber muffin to boost fiber.
children.nutrition_note: Approx 320–420 kcal; protein 15–20 g; carbs 30–40 g; fat 9–12 g. Fiber from the whole-wheat muffin; tomato adds micronutrients. Sodium varies with cheese; consider lower-sodium cheddar to reduce salt.

children.name: Breakfast Wrap — Smoky Variation
children.short_description: Smoky breakfast wrap with egg, turkey, spinach in a whole-wheat tortilla and salsa.
children.meal_suggestion: For lower sodium, use low-sodium turkey and salsa; add avocado for healthy fats to enhance fullness and flavor.
children.nutrition_note: Balanced breakfast wrap with lean protein (turkey and egg) and fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach. Monitor sodium from turkey and salsa; consider low-sodium options if needed.

children.name: Turkey Sandwich — Classic Set
children.short_description: Classic turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard.
children.meal_suggestion: Pair with a piece of fruit and a small yogurt for a complete lunch; add extra veggie slices or a side salad to boost fiber.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich turkey and fiber from whole-grain bread provide a balanced base with vitamins from lettuce and tomato. Low in fat; sodium can be moderate—opt for low-sodium turkey and mustard if salt is a concern.

children.name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Classic Variation
children.short_description: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Classic variation with chicken, rice, mixed vegetables, and teriyaki sauce.
children.meal_suggestion: Aim for a balanced portion: 3–4 oz cooked chicken, 1 cup cooked brown rice, and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables. Use low-sodium teriyaki or dilute the sauce with water, and finish with sesame seeds or sliced green onions for flavor without excess calories.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken supports satiety; rice provides energy; mixed vegetables add fiber, vitamins, and minerals. Teriyaki sauce contributes flavor but can add sodium and added sugars. For a healthier option, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa, and use a low-sodium or diluted sauce to reduce sodium intake.

children.name: Grilled Cheese & Soup — Lemon-Herb Variation
children.short_description: Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, served with tomato soup and grapes—a simple, fiber-rich meal with protein, calcium, and fruit.
children.meal_suggestion: For a lighter option, use low-sodium tomato soup and/or reduced-fat cheddar; keep the whole-grain bread for fiber. Pair with a side salad or steamed veggies to boost micronutrients and volume without excessive calories.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread and grapes; calcium and protein from cheddar; tomato soup adds vegetables. Moderate sodium from cheese and soup; consider low-sodium tomato soup and/or reduced-fat cheese to lower sodium and saturated fat. The lemon-herb variation isn't reflected in the listed ingredients; to capture that flavor, add lemon zest and herbs to the sandwich or soup.

children.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Lemon-Herb Bowl
children.short_description: Plant-based meal: vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad—light, fiber-rich, and satisfying.
children.meal_suggestion: Boost protein by adding beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or salad; use a low-sodium soup; drizzle the salad with olive oil and lemon for healthy fats.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich and vitamin-packed with vegetables and whole-grain bread; protein is moderate; sodium varies by soup; opt for low-sodium soup for heart health.

children.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Mild-Spice Style
children.short_description: Mild-spice style stuffed bell peppers filled with lean ground turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a complete meal. Pair with a side salad or steamed greens to boost fiber; substitute brown rice for more texture and nutrition; adjust cheese amount for a lighter or richer flavor.
children.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides high-quality protein; rice supplies quick energy carbs; bell peppers add fiber and vitamin C. Cheddar adds calcium and flavor, contributing some saturated fat and sodium depending on cheese and sauce. For a lighter version, choose low-sodium tomato sauce and lean turkey.

children.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Combo
children.short_description: Tofu and mixed vegetables in curry sauce over rice, a quick, plant-based curry meal with citrusy freshness.
children.meal_suggestion: For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or serve with a side salad; add a squeeze of lemon or lime for extra citrus brightness.
children.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein from tofu, fiber from mixed vegetables, and carbohydrates from rice. The curry sauce adds flavor and sodium; consider portion control or choosing a low-sodium option if needed.
